creator: oberman@es.net
text:
> Date: Tue, 26 Sep 2006 19:14:04 -0300
> From: Rainer Alves <rainer.alves@gmail.com>
> 
> Hi Kevin, sorry for the late response.
> I've been unable to reproduce this error here...
> 
> [rainer@bsd ~]$ pkg_which /usr/local/lib/libgthread-2.0.so.0
> glib-2.10.3
> 
> Have you tried rebuilding devel/glib20 ?
> Are you using a different threading library (for instance libthr by 
> means of /etc/libmap.conf) ?

It is happening on both of my local systems, so I don't completely
understand why it's not everywhere. Are you running Gnome or do you just
have GTK2 and glib installed? I have a very full-blown 2.14 with most
all the trimmings, although I don't see the reason that should make a
difference.

FWIW, this probably belongs to the maintainer of p5-Gtk2 as that is the
where the binary that dies is coming from. There are only two binaries
in dvdrip and they are not used until long after the point where it
dies. Clearly, something is not built with the proper thread library
linkages and p5-Gtk2 is the obvious suspect.

At this time I have no other applications using the Perl GTK2 bindings,
so I would not see this in any other port.

Thanks for looking at it, but could you try getting it re-assigned to
the p5-Gtk2 maintainer, clsung@? 
--
